title: Course: COSC 385 - Theory of Languages and Automata
description: Introduction to automata theory, languages, syntax, and grammar characteristics. Topics include mathematical preliminaries, the Chomsky hierarchy, finite automata, regular expressions, pushdown automata, context-free languages, and Turing machines.
prerequisites_text: COSC 220 and COSC 281 with grades of C or higher
content:
COSC 385 - Theory of Languages and Automata
Credits: 3
Prerequisites: COSC 220 and COSC 281 with grades of C or higher
Offered: As needed

Description: Introduction to automata theory, languages, syntax, and grammar characteristics. Topics include mathematical preliminaries, the Chomsky hierarchy, finite automata, regular expressions, pushdown automata, context-free languages, and Turing machines.

Curriculum role:
  - BS Computer Science: Group B
  - BS Cloud Computing: Group B

Catalog page: https://catalog.morgan.edu/preview_course_nopop.php?catoid=26&coid=55599
